diagrams.title: Structure Definition and Declaration Visual
diagrams.description: Shows how a structure is defined using 'struct' keyword and how structure variables are declared
elements: Variable declaration: struct Student s1, s2;
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the syntax and components of structure definition

diagrams.title: Structure vs Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Compares memory layout of an array (same type) versus a structure (different types)
elements: Array: contiguous blocks of same size
Structure: contiguous blocks of different sizes
Shows member names and their data types
Demonstrates why structure is used for heterogeneous data
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the difference between array and structure storage

diagrams.title: Structure Member Access Methods
diagrams.description: Shows two ways to access structure members: dot operator for variables and arrow operator for pointers
elements: Dot operator: s1.rollno (s1 is a variable)
Arrow operator: ptr->rollno (ptr is a pointer)
Memory representation showing pointer pointing to structure
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when to use dot vs arrow operator for member access

diagrams.title: Structure Padding Illustration
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how compiler adds padding bytes to structure for memory alignment
elements: Added padding bytes shown in different color
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why sizeof(struct) may be larger than sum of member sizes
